info.system: Answer with compact JSON only. Do not call tools.

parts.text:
You are given the full prior transcript bundle below.
Use it to answer the provenance question and cite the provided source ids.

<session id=auth_refresh_session title=Auth refresh queue rationale>
<message id=auth_fact_1>The rejected design was one global mutex around all auth refresh work.</message>
<message id=auth_fact_2>The global mutex was rejected because auth_refresh_different_tenants_parallel showed unrelated tenants must not block each other.</message>
<message id=auth_fact_3>The chosen design is RefreshQueue with a per-tenant key: same-tenant duplicate refreshes coalesce, while different tenants continue in parallel.</message>
</session>
<session id=billing_retry_session title=Billing retry queue rationale>
<message id=billing_fact_1>Billing retry queues used a global mutex because the billing provider serializes all tenants.</message>
</session>
<session id=markdown_parser_session title=Markdown parser onboarding cleanup>
<message id=docs_fact_1>Markdown parser cache issues affected onboarding docs and quickstart text.</message>
</session>

Question: Why does src/auth/queue.ts deduplicate refresh work per tenant instead of using one global mutex?
Return compact JSON only with this shape:
{"answer":"...","evidence":{"session_id":"...","blob_id":"...","message_id":"..."},"why_not_global_mutex":"...","irrelevant_context_ignored":["..."]}
The answer must cite the exact source identifiers you used. If a field is unavailable, use an empty string.

**Considering JSON structure**

I see we need to create a compact JSON format without using any tools. I should include evidence, possibly just one object, and focus on shaping that evidence correctly. It's important to cite the exact source IDs as well. I should also clarify the reason behind the answer, while ignoring anything irrelevant like billing or documentation details. Keeping everything concise and to the point will be key. Let's make this clear and structured!

{"answer":"src/auth/queue.ts deduplicates refresh work per tenant because same-tenant duplicate refreshes should coalesce, but different tenants must continue in parallel.","evidence":{"session_id":"auth_refresh_session","blob_id":"","message_id":"auth_fact_3"},"why_not_global_mutex":"A single global mutex was rejected because auth_refresh_different_tenants_parallel showed unrelated tenants must not block each other.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["billing_retry_session: billing uses a global mutex for a different reason, because its provider serializes all tenants","markdown_parser_session: onboarding docs and quickstart cache issues are unrelated to auth refresh queuing"]}
